Title: An ancient role for CYP73 monooxygenases in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and embryophyte development

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as a critical and evolutionarily conserved control step; efficient flux-control point discussed, but not directly proven rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 4-hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms key upstream precursor for downstream phenylpropanoids and cuticular phenolic monomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Soluble extracts and cuticular polymer of bryophyte gametophores/thalli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports embryophyte development and contributes to cuticular polymer formation/barrier properties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present only in embryophytes, not detected as bona fide homologs in surveyed charophytes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Piperonylic acid treatment inhibits C4H function; exogenous p-coumaric acid rescues moss developmental defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; inhibitor treatment; mutant phenotyping; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or inhibition of CYP73 causes phenylpropanoid shortage and developmental defects across bryophytes/embryophytes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP73 family emerged in an embryophyte progenitor together with bona fide C4H activity. 2. CYP73 proteins from moss, liverwort and hornwort convert trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in vitro. 3. Impairing CYP73 function decreases phenylpropanoids and disrupts development and cuticle-associated metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CYP73 deficiency causes severe growth and developmental defects and cuticle impairment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to modulate phenylpropanoid flux, but strong pleiotropic developmental penalties caution against simple loss-of-function
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression data from public databases; BioStudies: S-SCDT-10_1038-S44318-024-00181-7
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies a conserved arginine required for activity and supports CYP73 as a foundational enzyme of the canonical phenylpropanoid pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical upstream control step; not individually proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursor for p-coumaroyl and caffeoyl esters and cuticular phenolic monomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Moss gametophores; soluble phenolic esters; cuticular polymer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to cuticle formation and normal gametophore/phyllid development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Prominently expressed in P. patens tissues; expressed in gametophores, especially stem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GUS reporter showed expression in gametophore stem and overlapping domains with PpCYP73A49
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous p-coumaric acid rescues double-mutant phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 complementation; homologous recombination knockout; GUS reporter; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional C4H paralog contributing redundantly with PpCYP73A49 to phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and moss development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP73A48 converts trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in vitro. 2. It complements the Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 mutant. 3. Single knockout shows little phenotype, indicating redundancy with PpCYP73A49. 4. Combined loss with PpCYP73A49 abolishes C4H-derived phenylpropanoids and causes severe developmental defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined disruption with PpCYP73A49 causes stunted growth, phyllid fusion and protonema overproduction
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis CYP73A5 promoter, constitutive/native-like for complementation in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering early phenylpropanoid flux in bryophytes, but redundancy and severe developmental penalties of strong impairment must be considered
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq from CoNekT; BioStudies: S-SCDT-10_1038-S44318-024-00181-7
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residue R225 is crucial for substrate binding and catalytic activity based on docking and mutagenesis.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical upstream control step; not individually proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursor for p-coumaroyl and caffeoyl esters and cuticular phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Moss gametophores; soluble phenolic esters; cuticular polymer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to cuticle barrier properties and normal moss organ development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Prominently expressed; extends to apex and basal parts of phyllids in gametophores
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GUS reporter detected in stem, apex and basal phyllid regions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1 complementation; homologous recombination knockout; GUS reporter; metabolite profiling; permeability assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major functional C4H paralog whose loss reduces specific phenylpropanoids, alters cuticle composition and increases permeability; acts redundantly with PpCYP73A48 for full pathway output
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCYP73A49 converts trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in vitro and complements Arabidopsis cyp73a5-1. 2. Single knockout causes thinner, fragile phyllids and reduced caffeoyl-related metabolites. 3. It alters cuticle composition and increases toluidine blue permeability. 4. Double knockout with PpCYP73A48 abolishes downstream phenylpropanoids and causes severe developmental defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes cuticle barrier defects and developmental abnormalities
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis CYP73A5 promoter, constitutive/native-like for complementation in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune cuticle-associated phenylpropanoid metabolism, but loss impairs barrier function and development
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq from CoNekT; BioStudies: S-SCDT-10_1038-S44318-024-00181-7
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutant accumulated trans-cinnamic acid and showed reduced caffeic acid upon saponification.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical upstream control step; partial redundancy due to lowly expressed paralogs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursor for shikimate esters and cuticular phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Liverwort thalli; soluble extracts; cuticular polymer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports thallus growth, gemma cup formation and cuticular polymer formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primary CYP73 paralog by expression in M. polymorpha tissues; much higher than MpCYP73A2/A3 in thallus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest among CYP73 paralogs in 3-week-old thalli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Piperonylic acid aggravates mutant-like phenotype in M. polymorpha
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; in vitro enzyme assay; CRISPR mutagenesis; metabolite profiling; cuticular polymer analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss reduces key phenylpropanoid intermediates, decreases cuticular polymer quantity and strongly impairs thallus expansion and gemma cup formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpCYP73A1 is the predominant liverwort CYP73 paralog and catalyzes C4H activity in vitro. 2. CRISPR disruption causes strong developmental defects. 3. Mutants show ~50% reduction in p-coumaroyl-5-O-shikimate and caffeoyl-5-O-shikimate. 4. Cuticular polymer composition and abundance are reduced, but pathway output is not completely abolished because of residual paralog redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes dramatic reduction in thallus area and gemma cup number
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: MpU6-1 promoter used for gRNA expression in CRISPR/Cas9 editing
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central engineering target for liverwort phenylpropanoid metabolism, but partial redundancy and strong developmental defects caution against full knockout
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq from CoNekT and MarpolBase; BioStudies: S-SCDT-10_1038-S44318-024-00181-7
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutants accumulated trans-cinnamate in cuticular polymer and had reduced ferulate.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports inference that hornworts possess conserved C4H step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected based on best full-length RNA-seq scaffold quality in 1kP database
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay in yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Demonstrates that hornwort CYP73 enzyme conserves C4H activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcCYP73A catalyzes p-coumaric acid production from trans-cinnamic acid in vitro. 2. This supports conservation of C4H activity across bryophyte lineages.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoter in yeast expression plasmid pYeDP60
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative enzyme for evolutionary engineering studies of early phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No in planta hornwort gene knockout was performed; hornwort developmental evidence comes from piperonylic acid treatment, not direct PcCYP73A mutagenesis.
